message:
fix(core): skip pager-level memdb reload inside explicit transactions

prepare_streaming_join_select_read_boundary unconditionally reloaded
the in-memory schema from the pager's published snapshot for
file-backed connections.  Inside an explicit transaction (BEGIN
EXCLUSIVE), DDL changes like CREATE TABLE have not yet been committed
to the pager, so the reload overwrote the active schema with the
pre-transaction state — causing INSERT INTO after CREATE TABLE to
fail with "no such table".

This broke beads_rust's rebuild_issues_table which executes:
  BEGIN EXCLUSIVE
  CREATE TABLE issues_rebuild_tmp (...)
  INSERT INTO issues_rebuild_tmp SELECT ... FROM issues
  DROP TABLE issues
  ALTER TABLE issues_rebuild_tmp RENAME TO issues
  COMMIT

The INSERT...SELECT hit the streaming join fallback path, which
called prepare_streaming_join_select_read_boundary, which reloaded
from pager, which lost issues_rebuild_tmp.

Fix: add an early return when self.in_transaction is true.  Inside an
explicit transaction, the active-txn refresh at the top of the
function (lines 15467-15473) already handles reloading from the
transaction's own pages correctly.  The pager-level reload must be
skipped to avoid clobbering uncommitted DDL.

Verified: all 150 beads_rust storage tests pass, all fsqlite-core
insert_select/retained_autocommit/create_table tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
